creator: jlh@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, May 29, 2013 at 04:50:49PM -0500, Bryan Drewery wrote:
> The fallout is not too bad.

Excellent, I'm glad to hear that!


> Control run:
>   http://pb2.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/91i386-default/2013-05-28_11%3A59%3A14/
> libc.ld run:
>   http://pb2.nyi.freebsd.org/bulk/9i386-ld-default/2013-05-27_12%3A48%3A15/
> 
> libc.ld new failures:
>   devel/meta-cvs
>   lang/mono
>   games/epiar
>   security/openssl
>   www/mod_spdy (embedded openssl)
>   databases/firebird21-server (likely unrelated, firebird20 fails in
> control due to other issues)

So I guess the more important to fix is security/openssl for which you
already have a fix, right?  That way we will probably be able to test
most of the skipped ports.

Do you want to submit your openssl fix before I commit the patch to the
tree?

Thanks again for doing this. :)
-- 
Jeremie Le Hen

Scientists say the world is made up of Protons, Neutrons and Electrons.
They forgot to mention Morons.
